channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) and pan-Yoruba socio-political organisation, Afenifere, have commended the rescue of the pupils The post ADC, Afenifere laud rescue of kidnapped pupils, teachers appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Chichi Nwoko Urges Corporate Nigeria To Invest in Team Nigeria’s Sporting Future
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Chief Executive Officer of What Now Media Group, Chichi Nwoko, has called on corporate organisations and private sector stakeholders to play a more active role in Nigeria’s sporting renaissance by investing in the nation’s athletes and supporting the long-term development of sports. Nwoko said N

item.title: Hannah Elliott Highlights Tinnitus Risks
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Bloomberg Pursuit staff writer Hannah Elliott says Tinnitus affects more than 50 million Americans, remains underdiagnosed and underfunded, and can have profound effects on mental health and quality of life. She says growing awareness of hearing health and new treatments, including the FDA-cleared L

item.title: Lagos schools benefit as firms roll out massive book distribution
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Two companies, Academy Press Plc and Ekenua & Co. Limited, have donated 1,500 copies of George Orwell’s famous novel, Animal Farm, to students in three senior secondary schools in Ilupeju, Lagos. The donation is part of a new education project called the Million Classics Programme, which a

item.title: SBM flags rising kidnap threats, mob justice as armed groups shift across Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Security intelligence firm SBM Intelligence has flagged rising kidnap threats and mob justice as armed groups shift across Nigeria. The post SBM flags rising kidnap threats, mob justice as armed groups shift across Nigeria appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: Is it better to spend my savings now so I can delay taking Social Security? How do I choose?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “By claiming earlier, I could preserve more of my portfolio and allow those assets to continue compounding.”

item.title: Wembanyama Signs New $252M Lonf-Term Deal With Spurs
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Wembanyama has signed a long-term deal with the San Antonio Spurs. The 7ft 4in France centre’s deal, according to ESPN, is for five years and worth $252m (£188m), which is the NBA’s maximum for a rookie scale player extension. The 22-year-old reportedly has an option in the fifth season

item.title: PT Health Watch: What expectant parents should know about Rhesus incompatibility
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Rhesus incompatibility is almost entirely preventable through standard, modern prenatal care. The post PT Health Watch: What expectant parents should know about Rhesus incompatibility appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Rescued Oyo victims undergoing medical checks, will be handed to Makinde – Presidency
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Presidency said on Saturday that the children and teachers rescued from Oriire community in Ogbomoso, Oyo State, are currently undergoing medical examinations. The post Rescued Oyo victims undergoing medical checks, will be handed to Makinde – Presidency appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: “Nobody Wants Peter Obi Dead, But Alive To Lose 2027 Election” – Dave Umahi
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Minister of Works David Umahi has dismissed the death threat statement credited to the presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi, describing the statement as a sign of frustration. Umahi warned Obi to stop verbal attacks on President Bola Tinubu or he would also atta

item.title: Bandits ends teacher’s life after collecting N3m ransom from family
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A teacher, Malam Mustapha Yahaya Maru, who was abducted along the Anka Road in Zamfara State, has reportedly been killed by his captors despite the payment of a N3 million ransom by his family. The development was disclosed by journalist Bakatsine, who reports

item.title: Education Spending: Kano beats 207 other regions, ranked best in Africa
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Kano State has been ranked as the top sub-national government in West Africa for spending on education, according to the 2026 Sub-National Education Spending Index released by the University of Paris. The report assessed 209 states, regions, districts and municipalities across the 15 member countrie

item.title: 2027 Poll: Labour Party Unveils Female Vice Presidential Candidate
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Labour Party has unveiled Hajja Bintu Konto as its vice presidential candidate for the 2027 general election, pairing her with the party’s presidential candidate, Dr. Chibuzo Okereke, as it positions itself ahead of the next presidential contest. The announcement was made in a statement issued o

item.title: Colourful, controversial and significant - ex-Chelsea and Leeds owner Bates dies
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Former Chelsea and Leeds owner Ken Bates, who has died aged 94, was a colourful and controversial figure - but also a significant one for English football, writes Phil McNulty.

item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football

In a statement issued on Saturday by the Director of Media and Public

item.title: Cuba suffers second nationwide blackout in five days
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Cuba suffered its second nationwide blackout in five days on Friday as twin energy and economic crises, worsened by a six-month US fuel blockade, continue to cripple the country's electricity grid. "The pressure is increasing," explains our journalist Shirli Sitbon.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has approved the conversion of the Federal Polytechnic, Nasarawa, into the Federal University of Mining, Engineering and Technology, Nasarawa, in a move aimed at strengthening technical education and advancing Nigeria’s solid minerals sector. The approval was announced in a sta

item.title: We will support Oriire victims until full recovery, says Makinde
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: He wrote: "Earlier today, we visited the victims of the Oriire LGA kidnapping incident where they are receiving treatment in Ibadan. We will continue to provide the necessary support needed for their full recovery and reunite them with their families."

item.dc:creator: PM News

item.title: An islandwide blackout strikes Cuba for the second time this week
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: An islandwide blackout struck Cuba on Friday for the second time this week as the nation of nearly 10 million people grapples with a crumbling power grid and fuel shortages stemming from a U.S. energy blockade.

item.title: McCaul Backs Deeper US Ukraine Defense Ties
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets

Michael McCaul says Ukraine is gaining momentum on the battlefield but urgently needs more Patriot interceptors and stronger air defenses to counter Russia's escalating missile attacks. He urges deeper US-Ukraine defense cooperation, including licensed Patriot production and joint drone develop

item.title: Why rescued Oyo pupils are yet to rejoin their families — Makinde
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Governor Seyi Makinde of Oyo State has said the rescued schoolchildren and teachers from Oriire Local Government Area will remain in military custody for medical attention and routine screening. The post Why rescued Oyo pupils are yet to rejoin their families — Makinde appeared first on The Sun Nige

item.title: Overnight Russian missile and drone strikes on Ukraine kill six, officials say
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Russian attacks on Ukraine killed six people, including a child, and wounded dozens more, according to local officials on Saturday. Moscow launched more than 120 drones and 12 missiles during the night, according to Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, putting his country's air defence system und

item.title: World Cup: ‘He can’t stop Haaland’ – Sam Allardyce tells Tuchel to drop England star against Norway
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former England manager Sam Allardyce has advised Thomas Tuchel to drop Marc Guehi, a teammate of Erling Haaland at Manchester City, from the lineup for the quarter-final match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. In an interview with Metro, Allardyce emphasized that Guehi should not be tasked with marking th

item.title: Belgian rider Tim Merlier wins eighth stage of Tour de France in bunch sprint
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Belgian rider Tim Merlier secured his second straight stage victory at the Tour de France on Saturday, dominating a bunch sprint finish in the eighth stage.
